title: Beyond Simple Accuracy
concept: Accuracy can be a misleading metric, especially on imbalanced datasets where rare classes are ignored.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary purpose of calibrating a model's confidence scores?

options.text: To align the stated confidence scores with the actual probability of being correct.
To increase the model's accuracy on the validation set.
To reduce the physical storage size of the weights.

exercises.explain: Correct! Calibration ensures confidence represents realistic probability, which is vital for safe decision-making.

cards.title: The broken smoke alarm
cards.body: If you buy a smoke alarm that is labeled '99.9% accurate', that sounds amazing. But if the alarm achieves this simply by never going off (because fires are rare), it is useless when a real fire starts. Simple accuracy hides this failure.

cards.title: The limit of accuracy
cards.body: Accuracy is defined as Correct Predictions / Total Predictions. While intuitive, accuracy is a highly misleading metric on imbalanced datasets where one class dominates the data.

cards.title: A lazy student who says 'No'
cards.body: Think of a student who takes a test where 99% of the answers are 'False'. Without reading any questions, the student writes 'False' for everything. They get a 99% score, but they didn't learn a single thing.

cards.title: Rare disease detection
cards.body: A medical dataset has 1,000 patient scans, but only 10 have lung cancer. A model that predicts 'Healthy' for all 1,000 patients achieves 99% accuracy, but it misses 100% of the cancer cases. This model is a failure.

cards.title: High accuracy does not mean high performance
cards.body: You might think a model with 98% accuracy is ready for production. In reality, you must check the distribution of your test classes first. If the target class represents only 2% of your data, a 98% accurate model might just be guessing the majority class constantly.

cards.title: Accuracy on Imbalanced Data
cards.body: Why accuracy fails when classes are 99 to 1:

left.text:
990 safe transactions
Model predicts 'Safe' for all
990 correct predictions

right.text:
10 fraud transactions
Model predicts 'Safe' for all
10 incorrect predictions (100% missed)

exercises.prompt: Why is simple accuracy a poor metric for evaluating models trained on highly imbalanced datasets?
exercises.explain: A naive model that always predicts the dominant class will look highly accurate, while failing completely on the minority class.

options.text: Because a model can achieve very high accuracy by simply predicting the majority class for all inputs.
Because accuracy cannot be computed on decimal numbers.
Because it requires training on twice as many epochs.
Because it forces the logits to sum to infinity.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A model with 99% accuracy on a dataset where 1% of samples are positive is guaranteed to be highly effective at detecting positive samples.
exercises.explain: False. The model could achieve 99% accuracy by predicting 'negative' for all samples, detecting zero positive samples.
Accuracy measures the fraction of all predictions that the model got correct.
exercises.sentence: Accuracy is computed as correct predictions divided by ___ predictions.

exercises.prompt: Which of these datasets will make accuracy a misleading metric for model evaluation?
exercises.explain: A 95/5 ratio is highly imbalanced, which makes simple accuracy a poor measure of performance.

options.text: A dataset with 9,500 healthy scans and 500 tumor scans
A dataset with 5,000 dog photos and 5,000 cat photos
A dataset with equal numbers of five different fruit species

exercises.prompt: You are building a defect classifier for a microchip factory. Only 0.1% of manufactured chips are defective. A model always predicts 'No Defect'. What is its accuracy, and is it a useful model?

options.text: Accuracy is 99.9%. It is completely useless because it fails to catch any defective chips.
Accuracy is 0.1%. It is useful because it is honest.
Accuracy is 50%. It is useless because it is random.
Accuracy is 100%. It is perfect for production.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The model achieves 99.9% accuracy, but it is an absolute failure for the business because the defective chips will pass review and ruin products. We need better metrics! Next, let's explore Precision, Recall, and F1-Score!
